description: validate_result silently checks the already-clipped 30-row history payload, not the 200-row execute_sql result. The agent loop calls _clip_for_history before re-injecting into the LLM, so the model passes the truncated dict to validate_result. numeric_ranges/non_null_columns/min_rows assertions all evaluate on the head of the result; any violation past row 30 (e.g. a negative PNL_SUM far down a sorted list) is silently accepted as ok=True.
reproducer: Verified: 200-row result with negative value at row 199 returns ok=True when validator gets clipped view; ok=False on full result.

description: _jsonify's precision-preservation branch is dead code. The condition `f if f == float(value) else str(value)` always evaluates True because both operands compute float(value) identically. Any Oracle NUMBER returned as a Decimal with >15 significant digits silently loses precision (coerced to float) instead of falling back to a string preserving the digits.
reproducer: Verified: Decimal('12345678901234567890.123456789') → float 1.2345678901234567e+19, but `f == float(v)` is True so str fallback never fires.

description: oracledb.connect is called with no tcp_connect_timeout / call_timeout. If Oracle DSN is unreachable or hung (the documented `docker start ai-analyst-oracle` step missed), every agent run blocks on system-default Oracle TCP timeout (~60s+) before the swallowed exception in _data_range_note, then again on the first execute_sql. The CLI provides no user-visible progress and no upper bound.
reproducer: code_ref points: oracledb.connect call lacks any timeout kwarg; oracledb supports tcp_connect_timeout but it is not passed.

description: inspect_schema does not actually inspect Oracle — it only reads the static metadata.py. If the real client_product table diverges (renamed/added/dropped columns, different types) the agent generates SQL against the static map and gets ORA-00904 'invalid identifier' at execute time, with no recovery path beyond model retries. The tool name and SPEC's 'описание таблицы из metadata.py + БД' framing imply schema reconciliation that isn't implemented.
reproducer: code_ref: describe_table returns get_table_metadata() from metadata.py with no `oracledb` user_tab_columns probe.

description: AI_ANALYST_RESPONSE_MODEL is required by Settings.from_env() and crashes startup if missing, but is never referenced anywhere in the agent. The SQL model is used for both tool-calling SQL generation and the final NL response, contradicting the SPEC's distinct AI_ANALYST_SQL_MODEL / AI_ANALYST_RESPONSE_MODEL contract.
reproducer: grep -rn response_model text2sql_agent shows only the config assignment; no consumer.

description: _validate_sql only blocks DML/DDL keywords but does not restrict the FROM target. SELECTs against dba_users, v$session, SYS.*, all_users, DBMS_LOB.* all pass validation — defense relies entirely on the Oracle role granted to dev_user. If the connecting role has any inherited SELECT_CATALOG_ROLE-style grant (common in dev XE setups), an LLM-generated query can exfiltrate session/user metadata.
reproducer: Verified: _validate_sql('SELECT * FROM dba_users') and SELECT * FROM v$session both ACCEPTED.

description: validate_result.numeric_ranges silently skips any value that is not int|float — including the str fallback that _jsonify intends to emit for high-precision Decimals (dead today, but latent), and including NUMBER values returned as Python int. More critically, bool flows through `isinstance(v, (int,float))` and triggers spurious min/max issues because True==1 in Python. With a hypothetical CASE WHEN that yields BOOLEAN-like 0/1 mixed with True from the driver, ranges flag noise.
reproducer: Verified: validate_result with rows [[True],[False]] and numeric_ranges F:{min:10,max:100} → ok=False, 'F=True < min=10'.

description: Every agent.run() opens a fresh Oracle connection for the unconditional MIN/MAX MONTH_DT probe in _data_range_note, even for questions unrelated to time ranges, plus a new connection for every subsequent execute_sql. No pooling, no caching. On the 5-question grader this means 5 extra startup probes; on a slow XE container each probe adds seconds of latency to the user-visible CLI response.
reproducer: code_ref: _data_range_note unconditionally invoked from run(); each run_query opens via _connect.

description: The OpenAI client call in the agent loop has no request_timeout and no max_retries override. If Ollama hangs (model loading, long generation), each of the up to 8 steps can block indefinitely, and Ctrl-C is the only escape. The top-level except in __main__ converts the eventual exception into exit 1 but there is no soft cap.
reproducer: code_ref: client.chat.completions.create call passes only model/messages/tools/tool_choice/temperature.

description: validate_result.numeric_ranges does `break` after the first out-of-range value per column. For multi-row top-N or per-month results where multiple rows violate bounds, only one is reported; the model gets a single-issue picture and may issue a localized fix that leaves the other violations in place.
reproducer: code_ref: explicit `break` inside the per-row loop in numeric_ranges section.

description: execute_sql truncates results to 200 rows but the system prompt does not require the model to add ORDER BY / FETCH FIRST itself. When the model issues e.g. `SELECT ORGANIZATION_NM, PNL_SUM FROM client_product WHERE MONTH_DT = ...` (no aggregate) and the table holds thousands of rows, the agent silently feeds 200 arbitrary rows to validate / final_answer; final_answer's 'top customer' answer is taken from an unordered, truncated slice.
reproducer: code_ref: run_query unconditionally fetchmany(201); model-side prompt says 'ORDER BY for top-N' but the system enforces nothing.
